title: Memory and Learning
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Memory is (0) one of the most fascinating aspects of human cognition, playing a crucial role in learning, identity, and daily functioning. Scientists have made significant progress in understanding how memories are formed, stored, and retrieved.||Memory is not a single entity but rather consists (9) of several different systems. Short-term memory holds information temporarily, usually for no more (10) than 20-30 seconds, while long-term memory can store information for years or even a lifetime. The process of converting short-term memories (11) into long-term ones is called consolidation, which often occurs during sleep.||Repetition and practice are essential (12) for effective learning because they strengthen neural connections in the brain. However, research has shown that spacing out study sessions over time is more effective (13) than cramming all at once. This is known as the spacing effect. Additionally, connecting new information (14) to existing knowledge helps create stronger memories.||Forgetting is actually a normal and sometimes beneficial process. Our brains filter out unnecessary information (15) to prevent overload. However, some people experience memory problems due to age, injury, or conditions like Alzheimer's disease. Scientists are working (16) to develop treatments that could prevent or reverse memory loss, offering hope for millions of people affected by these conditions.

example.text: Memory is (0) of the most fascinating aspects of human cognition.

questions.explanation: Verb 'consists of' means to be composed of different systems.
Comparison 'no more than' indicates a time limit.
Verb 'converting into' indicates transformation from one type to another.
Expression 'essential for learning' indicates purpose or function.
Comparative 'more effective than' compares two study methods.
Verb 'connecting to' indicates establishing a connection with existing knowledge.
Purpose infinitive: 'to prevent' indicates the goal of filtering.
Expression 'working to develop' indicates the goal of the work.
